rationale: The cited paper is used beyond background listing: it is discussed as a key prior empirical study on contractual completeness/incompleteness and appears in the paper's theory and hypothesis development. The authors explicitly use Ghosh and John (2005) to motivate how completeness may relate to later monitoring and enforcement in nonfranchise settings, then develop their own franchise-context expectations. They also cite it as a methodological precedent for their contract content-analysis approach and again in model-specification discussion, indicating meaningful conceptual and methodological influence.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on Ghosh and John (2005) as a prior foundation for studying contract completeness in interfirm governance, then extends that line to franchise channels with added ex ante and ex post governance mechanisms. It also adopts a similar contract-coding measurement approach while testing different downstream behavioral outcomes.
